title: Release Gates, Migration, Recovery, Support, and Defense · Behavior Case: Production Defense
summary: The team ships from generated volume, closes no playtest barriers, cannot identify an artifact, and treats reinstall as the recovery plan. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the production defense increment in production release, rollback, and residual-risk defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend player value, algorithms, accessibility, correctness, performance, security, packaging, recovery, and residual risk together.
Publish accessible controls, known limitations, diagnostics, safe reset, data location, support path, and accountable owners.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Production Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production release, rollback, and residual-risk defense.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-release-recovery-defense-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-release-recovery-defense-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_release_recovery_defense_5(release_gates: int = 12, passed_gates: int = 12, rehearsed_failures: int = 6, open_critical_barriers: int = 0, rollback_minutes: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if release_gates < 8 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-gate"
    if rehearsed_failures < 4 or open_critical_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_critical_barriers, "recovery-gap"
    if rollback_minutes < 0 or rollback_minutes > 15:
        return False, rollback_minutes, "rollback-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-maze-release"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend player value, algorithms, accessibility, correctness, performance, security, packaging, recovery, and residual risk together.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Production Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Production Defense model · Theory Production Defense, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1618 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend release evidence, migrations, recovery rehearsals, support ownership, and residual risk; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.
